strategies.title: Earth battlestaff — alch-floor arbitrage
strategies.thesis: Earth battlestaff persistently trades ~450-500gp below its high-alch floor (9300 - nature rune 140 = 9160). The GE market has a structurally negative bid-ask (latest quote -173gp) because sellers know the alch option exists. Buying at-or-below the latest low and high-alching captures a near-riskless spread up to buy_limit throughput. The 24h band (8659-8875) never breaches the floor; the alch mechanism is a hard price floor set by game design.
strategies.entry: Buy offer ≤ 8,661 gp (latest 5m low). High-alch immediately upon fill.
strategies.exit: High-alch for 9,160 gp net (highalch 9,300 minus nature rune 140). No GE sell leg.
strategies.horizon: 1 cast every ~3 sec (well within the 1,200 casts/hr cap); full 4h cycle consumes capital and refills at next 4h reset.
strategies.confidence_why: 24h history shows 25 hourly buckets all in band 8659-8875 (n=300+ obs), well below alch floor; per-item seasonality across 24 hours confirms the negative GE margin is structural (not a time-of-day effect). Alch-floor mechanism is permanent game design.
strategies.evidence: alch_screen id=1399: buy_at=8661, highalch=9300, nat_cost=140, alch_margin=499, buy_limit=18000, profit_per_limit=8982000. item_history 24h 1h-grain: avg_low 8664-8789 across 25 buckets. quote id=1399: high=8661, low=8661, margin=-173. liquidity id=1399 1h: vol5m_total=35028, n_buckets=10. lookup_item id=1399: highalch=9300, buy_limit=18000, members=true.
strategies.invalidation: Low price ≥ 9,100gp in any 5m bucket (market repriced to alch floor). Nature rune price rises >200gp (would close gap).
risks: update_risk — a Jagex alch-value change would collapse the floor
data_too_young — only ~26d of alch-floor stability observed
capital_concentration — single-name exposure uses entire 50M budget
strategies.paper_trade: Over next 3 cycles, record: actual fill rate at limit buy 8661, actual alch returns after nature-rune purchase, any 5m low ≥ 9100. If fills < 80% of offer or any floor breach, demote to watch.

strategies.title: Onyx bolts (e) — alch-floor arbitrage
strategies.thesis: Onyx bolts (e) is a high-value alch target (highalch 9000, nature rune 140 → net 8860). Market persistently prices it ~440gp below the alch floor across 24h (8429-8522 band, never approaching 8860). 11,000/4h buy_limit makes this a high-throughput alch play with similar mechanism to Earth battlestaff but on a different item (diversification within archetype).
strategies.entry: Buy offer ≤ 8,415 gp (latest 5m low). High-alch immediately upon fill.
strategies.exit: High-alch for 8,860 gp net (highalch 9,000 - nature rune 140). No GE sell leg.
strategies.horizon: 1 cast every ~3 sec; full 4h cycle consumes capital and refills at next 4h reset.
strategies.confidence_why: 24h history shows 25 hourly buckets all in 8429-8522 range (n=300+ obs), well below 8860 floor. Per-item seasonality shows negative GE margin in every UTC hour (-128 to -146) — structural discount is permanent.
strategies.evidence: alch_screen id=9245: buy_at=8456, highalch=9000, nat_cost=140, alch_margin=404, buy_limit=11000, profit_per_limit=4444000. item_history 24h 1h-grain: avg_low 8429-8495 across 25 buckets. quote id=9245: high=8456, low=8415, margin=-128. liquidity id=9245 1h: vol5m_total=28917, n_buckets=10. seasonality(hour) id=9245: all 24 UTC hours show negative GE margin (-128 to -146). lookup_item id=9245: highalch=9000, buy_limit=11000, members=true.
strategies.invalidation: Low price ≥ 8,810gp for two consecutive 5m buckets. Nature rune spike >200gp.
risks: thin_vol_window — vol5m spiked to 6899 in latest bucket, average is 2892, snapback could slow fills
strategies.paper_trade: Track fill rate on 8,415gp offer; if < 80% over 3 cycles OR any 5m low ≥ 8810, exit.

strategies.title: Blue dragon leather — passive spread flip
strategies.thesis: Blue dragon leather is a high-volume crafting by-product (~10,948 units/5m, one of the highest in the market). The persistent ~70-150gp post-tax spread reflects liquidity friction (crafter sell pressure vs. d'hide-buyer demand) rather than any transient event. 24h history shows tight 2079-2247 band with the spread present in 88% of hourly buckets. The mechanism (steady crafter supply vs steady d'hide demand) is structural and self-reinforcing.
strategies.entry: Buy offer ≤ 2,126 gp (latest low). Place simultaneously with sell offer.
strategies.horizon: Each fill cycle: place buy, wait for fill (typically <30 min for 10k+ vol/5m), place sell, wait for fill (~30-90 min). Roughly 1 cycle per 4h block.
strategies.confidence_why: 24h 1h-grain history shows spread present in 22/25 hourly buckets (88%). top_flips shows it at rank #1 by filled_profit (864,892gp) with both legs fresh within 7 minutes. Steady crafter/d'hide supply-demand is structural.
strategies.evidence: top_flips filled_profit rank #1: id=2505 buy=2126 sell=2249 margin=79 buy_limit=13000 vol5m=10948. item_history 24h: avg_high 2103-2247, avg_low 2079-2209 across 25 buckets. quote id=2505: high=2249 high_age_s=433, low=2126 low_age_s=389. liquidity id=2505 1h: vol5m_total=14589. lookup_item id=2505: buy_limit=13000.
strategies.invalidation: Persistence < 60% over a 4h window. Realized spread collapses vs quoted margin (stale-print trap — screen spread_gap flags). Both legs go stale > 30min.
risks: self_impact — buying 13k units is 4% of 1h volume; selling another 4%; manageable but watch for slippage
data_too_young — only ~26d of consistent spread observed
strategies.paper_trade: Run one full cycle, record fill times for buy and sell offers, actual realized margin after tax. Compare against the 79gp quoted margin.

strategies.title: Stamina potion(4) — passive spread flip with trend tailwind
strategies.thesis: Stamina potion(4) is a high-volume PvM consumable (avg ~3879 units/5m). The 24h block-average history shows a clear upward drift: avg_high climbs 3498 → 3813 (+9% over 24h). The current ~105gp spread sits at the upper end of its typical 50-180gp range. Trade the persistent spread while it lasts, with a hard exit if the trend reverses. The trend tailwind boosts near-term confidence but raises reversal risk — hence medium (not high) confidence.
strategies.horizon: ~1-2h per leg given 3879 units/5m liquidity. Cycle ~3-4h.
strategies.confidence_why: 24h history shows spread persistent (in most buckets), but the clear upward price drift (+9% over 24h) means we're trading INTO a trend. n=24 hourly buckets supports the spread claim; trend duration is unproven beyond 24h.
strategies.evidence: top_flips rank #6 by filled_profit: id=12625 buy=3540 sell=3719 margin=105 buy_limit=2000 vol5m=3879. item_history 24h 1h-grain: avg_high 3395-3813 (uptrend), avg_low 3344-3647. quote id=12625: high=3719 high_age_s=61, low=3564 low_age_s=61. liquidity id=12625 1h: vol5m_total=17991. lookup_item id=12625: id=12625, buy_limit=2000.
strategies.invalidation: Close < 3,500gp (trend reversal), OR margin < 40gp for >2h, OR both legs go stale > 30min during active hours.
risks: update_risk — if Jagex adjusts stamina potion price/usage, demand shifts
trend_reversal — riding into an uptrend means a snap-back hits open inventory hard
strategies.paper_trade: Track price + spread over 4h. If price closes a 15m bucket < 3,500, immediately dump inventory at market. If margin persists < 40gp, demote.
